name: DeepeR Denoising Dataset
description: Raman spectral denoising dataset from DeepeR paper. Contains noisy input spectra and corresponding denoised target spectra for training deep learning denoising models.
citeAs: @article{Horgan_2021, title={High-Throughput Molecular Imaging via Deep-Learning-Enabled Raman Spectroscopy}, volume={93}, ISSN={1520-6882}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1021/acs.analchem.1c02178}, DOI={10.1021/acs.analchem.1c02178}, number={48}, journal={Analytical Chemistry}, publisher={American Chemical Society (ACS)}, author={Horgan, Conor C. and Jensen, Magnus and Nagelkerke, Anika and St-Pierre, Jean-Philippe and Vercauteren, Tom and Stevens, Molly M. and Bergholt, Mads S.}, year={2021}, month=nov, pages={15850--15860}}

distribution.name: DeepeR Denoising Dataset
distribution.description: Source data hosted at the original repository.

field.name: spectrum_id
field.description: Sequential integer index identifying each spectrum.

field.name: raman_shift_cm_inv
field.description: Raman shift wavenumber axis in cm⁻¹. Shape: (n_wavenumbers,).

field.name: spectrum_intensity
field.description: Raman scattering intensity in arbitrary units. Shape: (n_wavenumbers,) per spectrum.

field.name: target
field.description: Reference (clean or high-resolution) spectral intensity values, shape (n_wavenumbers,).

rai:dataCollection: 172,312 paired low-SNR (0.1 s) and high-SNR (1 s) confocal Raman spectra from n=11 hyperspectral Raman cell images of MDA-MB-231 breast cancer cells, acquired at 532 nm excitation. Spectra cover the fingerprint region (~600-1800 cm-1) with key bands at 795 cm-1 (nucleic acids), 1004 cm-1 (phenylalanine), 1300 and 1440 cm-1 (lipids), and 1660 cm-1 (amide I, proteins). Training augmentation included spectral flipping, shifting, and background subtraction.
rai:dataPreprocessingProtocol: Low-SNR (0.1 s) and high-SNR (1 s) spectra from the same pixel are paired directly. A 1D ResUNet was trained for 500 epochs using the Adam optimizer with an L1-norm loss function and a one-cycle learning rate scheduler. Eleven independent models were trained via 11-fold cross-validation (10 cell images for training/validation, 1 held-out test image per fold). The network achieved a mean MSE 10x lower than Savitzky-Golay filtering (mean PSNR 45.76, mean SSIM 0.9532 across 11 test folds).
rai:dataAnnotationProtocol: Each low-SNR spectrum (0.1 s integration time) is paired pixel-by-pixel with the corresponding high-SNR spectrum (1 s integration time) acquired from the same spatial location in the same hyperspectral Raman cell image. Ground-truth targets are defined purely by acquisition conditions; no manual annotation was performed.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: No personal or sensitive information. All spectra are from the MDA-MB-231 human breast cancer cell line cultured in vitro; no patient-derived primary specimens and no individual human identifiers are present.
rai:dataBiases: All 172,312 spectral pairs originate from a single cancer cell line (MDA-MB-231 breast cancer) imaged on one confocal Raman system at 532 nm. The denoising model learns noise characteristics specific to this instrument and the 0.1 s vs. 1 s integration-time regime; generalisation to other instruments, cell types, excitation wavelengths, or noise regimes requires revalidation. The 11 cell images used for 11-fold cross-validation may not capture the full range of cellular morphological variability.
rai:dataLimitations: The 1D ResUNet learns noise characteristics specific to the acquisition instrument and the 0.1 s/1 s integration-time pairing. Performance on other spectrometers, sample types, or noise levels is not guaranteed without retraining or fine-tuning. Dataset access is via a SharePoint link provided by the authors rather than a stable versioned repository, which may affect long-term reproducibility. The 11 cell images may not represent the full biological variability of the cell line.
rai:dataSocialImpact: A 10x improvement in Raman denoising over classical methods (Savitzky-Golay, wavelet, PCA) directly enables high-throughput label-free molecular imaging of cells, potentially accelerating drug screening, cancer diagnostics, and histopathology workflows that currently require prohibitively long acquisition times.
rai:dataUseCases: Training and benchmarking 1D deep learning denoising models for hyperspectral Raman imaging; comparison against classical spectral filters (Savitzky-Golay, wavelet, PCA); component of hybrid denoising + super-resolution pipelines for high-throughput Raman microscopy.
prov:wasGeneratedBy: Data collection: Laboratory spectroscopic measurements. Preprocessing: Raw noisy spectra are paired with high-SNR reference spectra of the same samples for supervised denoising model training. Annotation: Reference (clean) spectra are high-SNR acquisitions of the same samples obtained under longer integration times or reduced noise conditions.
